name: Al Nahdi Family Office
signals: The office manages the family's investment portfolio and subsidiaries, including the Nahdi Medical Company, which is listed on the Saudi Exchange and recorded $2.5 billion in revenues in 2024. Al Nahdi's other investments span multiple sectors, including transportation, education, catering, and other diversified investment assets.

name: Manaseer Group
signals: Founded in 1999 in Jordan, the Manaseer Group today has 21 subsidiaries, a $3.5 billion investment portfolio, and has generated $2 billion in revenue since 1999.

name: Hamad Al Sagri Holding Company (HHC)
signals: In 2024, Leejam Sports recorded $400.4 million in revenues and operates 223 centers across Saudi Arabia and the U.A.E. Al Sagri’s net worth was $1.1 billion as of March 2025.

name: Assila Investments
signals: Originally founded in 1970 as Mohammed Al-Issa and Sons (MASC) Holding Company, Assila Investments rebranded in 2010. The company is among the largest shareholders on the Saudi Exchange, owning 8% of Riyad Bank, 11.2% of Savola Group, and 22.2% of Taiba Investments as of April 2025.

name: O Capital Group
signals: In November 2024, the group’s subsidiary, O Tower, project developer and operator for the Mohammed VI Tower, announced the signing of a management agreement with Hilton for the hotel at the Mohammed VI Tower under the Waldorf Astoria brand, set to op As of April 2025, Benjelloun and his family’s net worth was $1.6 billion.

name: Power International Holding (PIH)
decisionMaker: Moutaz Al Khayyat; Ramez Al Khayyat

name: Olayan Financing Company (OFC)
signals: The Olayan Group is a subsidiary of Olayan Financing Company (OFC), founded in 1947 as the General Contracting Company. In April 2024, OFC and Lunate reached an agreement with the Investment Corporation of Dubai and Brookfield Corporation to acquire a 49% stake in ICD Brookfield Place, a commercial and retail property in Dubai.
position: Chair of the Executive Committee & Deputy Chair

name: Mansour Group
signals: The group has a diverse portfolio, including Man Capital, the Mantrac Group, the Al Mansour Holding Company for Financial Investments, and Manfoods-McDonald's Egypt. In December 2024, Mansour Automotive signed a technical licensing agreement with China's Shanghai Automotive & Industrial Corporation to manufacture and assemble MG cars in Egypt, with an initial capacity of 50,000 units annually.

name: Almarai
signals: Almarai expanded its food service division with a major acquisition in Egypt in 2024, and in December, it announced plans to invest $1.2 billion in pou

name: The Olayan Group
signals: The group’s investment portfolio spans global markets, and in late 2024, it announced several new high-impact investments in the technology and sustainable infrastructure sectors in Europe and North America.

name: Diyar Al Muharraq
signals: Diyar Al Muharraq is one of Bahrain’s largest integrated cities, and in late 2024, it completed several major residential and commercial sectors, including the expansion of Dragon City.

name: Al Ghurair Investment
signals: In late 2024, Al Ghurair Investment announced a strategic partnership with a global sustainable packaging firm to revolutionize its food packaging solutions across the GCC.

name: Barka Desalination Company
signals: Barka Desalination is a key infrastructure provider in Oman, and in 2024, it achieved a major sustainability milestone by reducing its carbon emissions by 20% through renewable energy integration.

name: Arada
family: Bin Sultan Al Qasimi; Alwaleed bin Talal
signals: Arada is the fastest-growing developer in the Northern Emirates, and in late 2024, it announced "Armani Beach Residences" on the Palm Jumeirah, its first project in Dubai.

name: Al Raid Group
decisionMaker: Sheikh Raed bin Mansour Al Araimi
signals: Al Raid Group is the developer of Al Araimi Boulevard, one of Oman's largest shopping and leisure destinations. In 2024, the group announced plans to launch a new eco-friendly luxury residential community in Muscat.

name: Fas Energy
signals: Fas Energy, a subsidiary of Fawaz Alhokair Group, is among the region’s largest developers of renewable energy projects. In 2024, it won two major solar project contracts in Saudi Arabia and Egypt, with a combined capacity of 1.5 gigawatts.

name: Qatari Diar
signals: Qatari Diar has over 50 projects under development across 20 countries. In late 2024, the company inaugurated Lusail Boulevard, a major commercial and leisure hub in Qatar, featuring dozens of global brands.

name: Watania Group
signals: Watania is one of the largest poultry and agricultural producers in Egypt, and in late 2024, it announced plans to expand its export market to Gulf countries and Europe.

name: Ezz Steel
signals: Ezz Steel is the largest steel producer in the Middle East and North Africa, and it recorded $2.5 billion in revenues in 2024. In 2024, the company announced a major investment plan to increase its production capacity and shift toward greener steel production technologies.

name: Al Safadi Group
signals: Al Safadi Group operates a successful chain of Lebanese restaurants across the U.A.E., and in 2024, it announced plans for a regional expansion into Saudi Arabia and Qatar.

name: Khalid bin Hamad Group
signals: In 2024, the group announced new investments in Bahrain’s tech and sports sectors, aimed at boosting international participation in the kingdom’s events.

name: Abu Dhabi National Hotels (ADNH)
signals: In 2024, ADNH completed the acquisition of several luxury hotels in Dubai and launched its own premium catering brand.

name: Al Jeraisy Group
signals: The group is among the largest office automation and IT solutions providers in Saudi Arabia, and in 2024, it signed a strategic agreement with a global technology giant to develop localized AI solutions for the public sector.

name: Ithra Dubai
signals: Ithra Dubai is the developer of the "One Za'abeel" mixed-use project, which features the world’s longest cantilever. In late 2024, the company launched the second phase of the Deira Enrichment Project, which aims to revitalize the historic Deira gold souk area.

name: G.P.C. Group
signals: G.P.C. Group is among the leading engineering and construction firms in Qatar, and in late 2024, it was awarded a major contract for the development of North Field gas facilities.

name: Oman Holding Company
signals: In 2024, the group announced a new mixed-use waterfront project in Muscat, featuring luxury residences and high-end retail space.

name: Arabian Construction Company (ACC)
signals: ACC has built some of the iconic buildings in the GCC, and in 2024, it was awarded two major skyscraper projects in Dubai and Riyadh, with a combined value of over $900 million.

name: Sama Jordan
signals: In 2024, Sama Jordan expanded its investments in Jordan’s tech and food sectors by launching a new digital payment platform and a sustainable food packagi

name: Al Ghurair Foods
signals: Al Ghurair Foods operates across milling, refining, and food manufacturing sectors. In November 2024, it launched a new range of plant-based protein products to meet the increasing demand for sustainable food solutions in the region.

name: El Araby Group
signals: El Araby Group is one of Egypt's largest manufacturers of home appliances and consumer electronics. In 2024, it signed a manufacturing agreement with a global technology firm to produce high-tech home appliances for the African and Middle Eastern markets.

name: Sobha Realty
signals: Sobha Realty has a multi-billion dollar project pipeline in Dubai and is among the region’s largest backward-integrated real estate companies. In late 2024, it launched Sobha Hartland II, an $8 billion project featuring high-end villas and luxury residences.

name: Besix Group
signals: Orascom Construction owns the Group, which is among the leading construction groups in the Middle East. In late 2024, Besix, in a joint venture, was awarded a $1.2 billion contract for the construction of a major infrastructure project in Saudi Arabia.

name: Apparel Group
signals: Apparel Group represents over 80 international brands and has over 2,100 stores across 14 countries. In early 2025, the group signed a licensing agreement with a global athleisure brand to launch 50 new stores across India and the GCC.

name: Almarai Company
decisionMaker: Prince Naif bin Sultan bin Mohammed bin Saud Al Kabeer
signals: Almarai is the world's largest vertically integrated dairy company and recorded $5.5 billion in revenues in 2024. In December 2024, Almarai announced an investment of $1.2 billion for the expansion of its poultry business in Saudi Arabia over the next five years.

name: Omran Group
signals: Omran Group is the Sultanate’s executive arm for tourism development and has a portfolio of over 30 hotels and mixed-use projects. In 2024, the group signed a partnership agreement with a global developer for a $2.4 billion integrated tourism project in Muscat.

name: Khalifa Juma Al Nabooda Group
signals: The group operates over 25 companies across automotive, construction, real estate, and hospitality. In 2024, Al Nabooda Automobiles launched two new luxury vehicle showrooms in Dubai and the Northern Emirates.

name: Bahwan CyberTek
signals: Bahwan CyberTek provides digital transformation solutions to the oil and gas, logistics, and banking sectors. In November 2024, the company launched its AI-driven predictive maintenance platform for energy companies in the GCC.

name: Al-Hazm Group
signals: Al-Hazm is a luxury retail and dining destination in Doha, and the group has expanded into real estate development and high-end hospitality. In 2024, the group launched "Al Hazm II," a $500 million expansion project featuring over 50 designer brands and premium restaurants.

name: BinDawood Holding
signals: BinDawood Holding operates two major retail brands, BinDawood and Danube, with over 80 stores across Saudi Arabia. In late 2024, the company acquired a French marketing tech firm to boost its digital transformation and personalize its customer loyalty programs.

name: Gharaffa Group
decisionMaker: Hamad bin Jassim bin Jaber Al Thani
signals: In 2024, the group announced a major new luxury residential and hospitality development in Doha, valued at $1.5 billion. As of April 2025, Al Thani’s net worth was $1.2 billion.

name: Astra Industrial Group
signals: Astra Industrial Group operates in five sectors: pharmaceuticals, specialty chemicals, power and steel, mining, and food and agriculture. In October 2024, its pharmaceutical arm, Tabuk Pharmaceuticals, signed a partnership agreement with a global biotech firm to manufacture advanced treatments for chronic diseases in Saudi Arabia.

name: Lulu Group International
signals: In 2024, Lulu Group opened its 270th hypermarket and announced plans to invest $1.1 billion in Saudi Arabia by 2026. The company is also expanding across Southeast Asia, with new projects underway in Malaysia and Indonesia.

name: Kandil Steel
signals: Kandil Steel exports to over 40 countries across Africa, Europe, and the Middle East. In 2024, the company announced an investment of $50 million to upgrade its cold rolling and galvanizing lines to meet the growing demand for flat-steel products in Egypt and regional markets.

name: Ali & Sons Holding
signals: In 2024, Ali & Sons Motors, the distributor for Audi, Volkswagen, and Porsche in Abu Dhabi, launched a new electronic vehicle showroom in the city. The group’s oil and gas subsidiary signed a multi-year service contract with ADNOC in late 2024 to provide precision engineering solutions for offshore projects.

name: Nesto Group
signals: Nesto Group has over 120 hypermarkets and supermarkets across the Middle East and India. In 2024, the business opened its largest fulfillment center in Sharjah, covering 500,000 square feet, to boost its e-commerce capabilities.

name: Alsulaiman Group
signals: Alsulaiman Group represents several international brands in the Middle East, such as IKEA, Flow, and Ehidna. In 2024, Alsulaiman Group and its logistics subsidiary, Flow, signed a strategic agreement with an e-commerce giant to manage its fulfillment and distribution operations in Saudi Arabia.

name: Sedar Global
signals: Sedar Global has over 80 showrooms across the Middle East and North Africa and is among the region’s largest window dressing and wallcovering providers. In October 2024, the company launched a new digital platform to offer AI-powered design consultations for its global customer base.

name: Tamimi Group
signals: Tamimi Group operates across 30 sectors, including retail, food distribution, construction, and power. In November 2024, Tamimi Markets, a subsidiary of the group, signed a joint venture agreement with Mitsui & Co. to develop advanced food production facilities in Saudi Arabia.

name: Ebrahim K. Kanoo
signals: In April 2025, Ebrahim K. Kanoo announced a partnership with a global supply chain technology company to digitize its logistics operations in Bahrain. In October 2024, it launched a new state-of-the-art service center for Toyota and Lexus in Bahrain, featuring advanced sustainable technologies.

name: The Commercial Real Estate Company (Al Tijaria)
signals: Al Tijaria recorded $104 million in revenues and had total assets of $1.8 billion in 2024. Its presence spans Kuwait, the U.A.E., Bahrain, and Oman, with a portfolio comprising hotels, residential real estate, malls, and mixed-use developments.

name: SARA Group
signals: SARA Group is a distributor of luxury lifestyle products and has over 70 showrooms across the Middle East. In 2024, the group entered into exclusive distribution agreements with several European luxury brands for the Saudi and U.A.E. markets.

name: Suntop Group
decisionMaker: Abdullatif Al Fozan; Khalid Al Fozan
signals: The Suntop Group, which traces its roots to the Al Fozan Group, has a diverse portfolio including building materials, metals, electronics, logistics, and real estate. In 2024, it expanded its footprint by launching a new furniture and home decor subsidiary and entered into a joint venture with a global logistics provider to enhance its distribution network in the GCC.
